entities.name: survodutide
entities.context: Survodutide, an investigational dual agonist that simultaneously targets both the glucagon receptor (GCGR) and GLP-1 R, has emerged as a promising therapeutic candidate for the comprehensive management of obesity and MASH.

entities.name: GLP-1 receptor
entities.context: Glucagon-like peptide-1 receptor (GLP-1 R) agonists have demonstrated remarkable effectiveness in the treatment of obesity and type 2 diabetes.

entities.name: glucagon receptor
entities.context: Survodutide, an investigational dual agonist that simultaneously targets both the glucagon receptor (GCGR) and GLP-1 R.

entities.name: hepatic fibrosis
entities.context: Although these agents provide beneficial effects for metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis (MASH) through their glucose-lowering and weight-reducing properties, their efficacy in promoting fibrosis regression remains unproven.

entities.name: Gastrointestinal Adverse Effects
entities.context: The drug is generally well tolerated, with primarily manageable gastrointestinal adverse effects.

Although these agents provide beneficial effects for metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis (MASH) through their glucose-lowering and weight-reducing properties, their efficacy in promoting fibrosis regression remains unproven.
